diff_quality.notes: Only App ID + Region are exposed, sourced from gitignored local.properties via BuildConfig; REST/auth keys stay server-side and login uses a backend-minted auth token — correct separation. Toolchain/manifest changes (minSdk 28, Kotlin 2.2.0, heap bump, Jetifier, ProGuard keep, call permissions, foreground-service types) are all justified by the CometChat v6 requirement and not gratuitous refactors. Evidence limitation: the actual integration source files (ChatService.kt, ConsultChatScreen.kt, ChatCallOverlay.kt, CometChatToken/AppointmentChatContext models) are referenced by the diff but their bodies are not included, so direct CometChat API usage could not be line-verified; build_pass=true is trusted as ground truth.

blockers.issue: Calls skill rule 1.8 documents `CometChatIncomingCall(modifier = ...)` with no `call` argument, which does not compile ('No value passed for parameter call'). The correct usage (a required `call =` driven by CometChat.addCallListener → StateFlow<Call?>) only appears in the compose-placement skill §7, so the two skills contradict each other.

The `Call` type FQN is never given as an importable import line; the calls/listener snippets imply `com.cometchat.chat.models.*`, but the real class is `com.cometchat.chat.core.Call` — the agent only found it by inspecting the resolved AAR after a compile error.
blockers.narrowing_reason: Reworded from 'not documented' to 'never given as an importable line'. The string com.cometchat.chat.core.Call DOES appear twice in the loaded calls skill (L147, L229), so the absolutist claim was false. The defensible core holds: no snippet anywhere gives an importable `import` line for it, and every neighbouring model import points at com.cometchat.chat.models.*, while the placement section's `Call?` snippet carries no imports at all.

blockers.issue: The core skill gives a conflicting Kotlin floor (≥2.1.0 vs ≥2.2.0). The resolved 6.0.x GA artifacts ship Kotlin 2.2.0 metadata and fail compileDebugKotlin under 2.1.20 with an 'incompatible metadata version' error, forcing a bump to 2.2.0.

Android calling stuck on 'connecting' (and not dismissed on cancel). ROOT CAUSE (same as web X1): CometChatCalls.init() is NEVER called on android — the WebRTC Calls SDK is uninitialized, so an accepted call can't establish a media session and hangs on 'connecting'. The call listener only sets/clears the incoming-call StateFlow; it does not host the ongoing/connecting session nor fully clear the connecting UI on onIncomingCallCancelled/onOutgoingCallRejected. NOT a build issue — the installed APK is current. Same incomplete-calling gap the agent left on web.
blockers.retag_reason: Re-tagged agent -> skills. The finding faults the AGENT for assuming the additive ringing path needs no CometChatCalls.init. That assumption came VERBATIM from the skills: cometchat-react-calls/SKILL.md:330 — 'Both canonical React v6 sample apps do calls this exact way with ZERO CometChatCalls.login/CometChatCalls.init' — and cometchat-android-v6-migration/SKILL.md:337 'Drop separate CometChatCalls.init', L387 'No CometChatCalls.init calls outside what V6 does internally'. The agent followed the guidance exactly and the guidance is wrong: fix aced4f6 added CometChatCalls.init(CallAppSettings) and calling then connected live. Filing this under `agent` blamed the integrator for a skills defect and under-reported the highest-value gap in the set.

blockers.issue: A2 — Android chat has NO way to INITIATE a call: CometChatMessageHeader hides the voice/video call buttons by DEFAULT in this UI Kit version, so the chat shipped with no call affordance. Confirmed live 2026-07-10; fixed by hideVoiceCallButton=false + hideVideoCallButton=false.

A3 — Android app returns to the HOME screen after a call ENDS: CometChatOngoingCallActivity is launched in its own task (FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK), so finishing it drops to the launcher (looks like the app 'exited'; process stays alive). Confirmed live 2026-07-10; fixed by re-foregrounding MainActivity (FLAG_ACTIVITY_REORDER_TO_FRONT) on CometChatCallEvent.CallEnded.

A4 — Android ghost call: CometChatOngoingCallActivity is NOT finished when the REMOTE party ends a 1:1 call. CometChat.CallListener.onCallEndedMessageReceived is a DEFAULT NO-OP that an app implementing only the four abstract ringing callbacks silently drops; CometChatEvents.callEvents CallEnded covers only the LOCAL end. Confirmed live.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Fix calls skill rule 1.8 so the `CometChatIncomingCall` example includes the required `call =` parameter and shows the CometChat.addCallListener → StateFlow<Call?> driving pattern, aligning it with compose-placement §7 (or cross-reference it).
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Calls skill rule 1.8 shows a `CometChatIncomingCall(modifier = ...)` signature that does not compile and contradicts compose-placement §7.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Document the fully-qualified `com.cometchat.chat.core.Call` type in the call-listener snippets instead of implying `com.cometchat.chat.models.*`.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: The `Call` FQN is undocumented and the implied package is wrong, forcing AAR inspection.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Replace the conflicting Kotlin floor guidance in the core skill with a single correct minimum (≥2.2.0 for 6.0.x GA), noting the 'incompatible metadata version' failure below it.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Core skill gives conflicting Kotlin floors (2.1.0 vs 2.2.0) and 2.1.20 fails to compile against 6.0.x GA.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Add an explicit note in the calls skill that `com.cometchat:calls-sdk-android` is a REQUIRED dependency alongside chatuikit for v6 calling (with the cloudsmith maven repo and version alignment), despite calling being marketed as bundled.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: calls-sdk-android is an undocumented required peer dep; without it the app crashes at init.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Add docs-mcp coverage for the backend Node/Express CometChat auth-token minting flow (create/sync user with role, mint auth token scoped to the verified session UID) so the agent isn't forced to reverse-engineer it from app source.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Backend auth-token flow was reconstructed from sibling ../backend source with no docs-mcp coverage of the expected Node backend flow.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: cometchat-android-v6-calls + cometchat-android-v6-compose-placement should show the exact Compose wiring to surface voice/video call buttons in the message header/consult screen, so 'calling in scope' yields a usable call entry point (not just a chat).
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Android chat has no calling option despite calling in scope (human-verified runtime).

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: cometchat-android-v6-calls must require CometChatCalls.init(CallAppSettingsBuilder{appId,region}) after login AND show the full connect flow: host the ongoing/connecting call session UI and clear it on end/cancel/reject. Mounting only the incoming-call overlay + a listener that tracks a flag gives calls that ring/connect-forever but never establish.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Android calls stuck on 'connecting'; Calls SDK never initialized; cancel doesn't dismiss (human-verified runtime).

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: State in the android calling skill that CometChatMessageHeader hides the voice/video call buttons by default and that hideVoiceCallButton=false + hideVideoCallButton=false (or a CometChatCallButtons component) is required to expose a call-initiate affordance.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Android chat shipped with no call button because the header hides them by default (A2).

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Warn in the android calling skill/docs that CometChatOngoingCallActivity runs in its own task and returns to HOME when a call ends, and show the FLAG_ACTIVITY_REORDER_TO_FRONT re-foreground workaround on CometChatCallEvent.CallEnded.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: Android app dropped to the home screen when a call ended (A3).

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Kit should finish the ongoing-call activity on a remote 1:1 end; at minimum the docs must flag that onCallEndedMessageReceived (a no-op default) MUST be overridden for remote-end teardown.
improvement_suggestions.motivated_by: A4 — Android ghost call: CometChatOngoingCallActivity is NOT finished when the REMOTE party ends a 1:1 call. CometChat.CallListener.onCallEndedMessageReceived is a DEFAULT NO-OP that an app implementing only the four abstract ringing callbacks silently drops; CometChatEvents.callEvents CallEnded covers only the LOCAL end. Confirmed live.

retracted.issue: Android chat shows no call buttons AT RUNTIME. CORRECTION (post-investigation): the code is actually correct — ConsultChatScreen renders CometChatMessageHeader (auto call buttons) + CometChat.addCallListener + the incoming-call overlay + the Calls SDK dependency. The buttons are absent because of the app-level calling layer (Voice & Video Calling not provisioned on the auto-created app, and/or the peer isn't online) — NOT an agent/skills code omission.  [UPDATE 2026-07-10, confirmed via live 2-client Maestro/adb test]: root cause CONFIRMED — CometChatMessageHeader HIDES the voice/video call buttons by default (hideVoiceCallButton/hideVideoCallButton default true). The prior 'code is correct' note was wrong: no call-initiate affordance shipped. Fixed by setting both to false. Reclassified SDK -> skills.
retracted.reason: Stale intermediate conclusion ('code is actually correct') that the report itself reversed; fully replaced by A2 (header hides call buttons by default). Kept for audit trail.

_meta.agent_final_summary:
## INTEGRATION SUMMARY

- **CometChat skill variants relied on:** `cometchat` dispatcher → `cometchat-android-v6-core`, `-compose-components`, `-compose-theming`, `-compose-placement`, and `-calls`. Backend contract came from reading the sibling `../backend` source, not from a skill.

- **Where skills were wrong / contradicted the SDK:**
  - `CometChatIncomingCall` signature: the **calls** skill (rule 1.8) shows a self-managing `CometChatIncomingCall(modifier = ...)` with no call argument — this **does not compile** (`No value passed for parameter 'call'`). The **compose-placement** skill (§7) was correct: it requires `call =` and must be driven by a `CometChat.addCallListener` → `StateFlow<Call?>` pattern, which is what I implemented.
  - `Call` package: not documented explicitly; the skills' listener snippet implies `com.cometchat.chat.models.*`, but the real FQN is `com.cometchat.chat.core.Call` (verified in the resolved chat-sdk AAR after a compile error).
  - Kotlin floor: the core skill gave conflicting floors (≥2.1.0 vs ≥2.2.0). Empirically the resolved 6.0.x GA classes need **2.2.0** metadata — 2.1.20 failed. I bumped to 2.2.0.

- **APIs I was unsure existed (candidate hallucinations) — all now compile-verified against the AARs:** `CometChatMessageHeader(user, hideBackButton=…)`, `CometChatMessageList(user=…)`, `CometChatMessageComposer(user=…)`, `CometChatTheme(colorScheme=…)` + `lightColorScheme()/darkColorScheme()`, `CometChatUIKit.loginWithAuthToken`, `UIKitSettingsBuilder().subscribePresenceForAllUsers().setEnableCalling(true)`, `CometChat.CallListener` overrides, `CometChatIncomingCall(modifier, call)`. I relied on the header's **built-in** call buttons rather than the standalone `CometChatCallButtons` (skill W4 flags it as broken; grep confirms zero usages).

- **Docs-escapes (left skills/docs-mcp):** none — no web searches. All symbol uncertainties were resolved by inspecting the resolved Gradle-cache AARs (`unzip`/`find`), which is the skills' own stated ground-truth method, not external docs. (docs-mcp was available but not needed.)

- **Scope completed vs incomplete:**
  - **Completed:** SDK init; backend-token login chained to app session + logout; appointment-scoped 1:1 chat surface; video/voice call entry (header buttons) + app-root incoming-call overlay; RBAC gating; secure credential handling; green `assembleDebug`.
  - **Not done (out of scope / not requested):** FCM/VoIP **push** for calls when the app is backgrounded (the in-app listener works foreground; background push wiring is a separate `-push` journey), and instrumented device-pair call testing (build-only verification per the gate — no emulator run). Both noted as follow-ups.
_meta.review_status: reviewed + human-runtime(android no calling option) + android X1 (calls-init gap, stuck connecting)

retracted_blockers.issue: V6 markets calling as 'bundled', but chatuikit bytecode-references CometChatCalls$SessionSettingsBuilder from calls-sdk-android; the calls-sdk-android artifact must be added as an explicit peer dependency or the app crashes at init. This required peer dep is not documented in the calls skill.
retracted_blockers.retraction_reason: MISDIAGNOSIS — the claimed documentation gap is the skill's MOST-documented item. The finding says the calls-sdk-android peer dep is undocumented; cometchat-android-v6-calls/SKILL.md carries it in the frontmatter description, the compatibility line, a dedicated section '### W1 — calls-sdk-android:5.0.+ is a REQUIRED peer dep' with the exact gradle line, plus the rationalizations table, red-flags table, verification checklist and anti-pattern #1. The run's skills_loaded INCLUDES cometchat-android-v6-calls, and the agent applied not only W1 but also W3 (the RTC stub classes appear in the diff), proving it read the workaround list. The technical fact is true; the stated CAUSE (a skills gap) is false.
